customManagers.description: Update Makefile tool versions via inline renovate comments
Update workflow env vars (e.g. ZAP_VERSION) via inline renovate comments — keeps the workflow literal in sync with the Makefile constant it duplicates
Track the Go toolchain pinned via GOTOOLCHAIN in Dockerfile.prod (CVE rebuild of the caddy binary)

Track the caddy version in the `xcaddy build vX.Y.Z` rebuild line of Dockerfile.prod so it bumps together with the caddy FROM digests (dockerfile manager) — otherwise Renovate bumps the FROM base while the rebuilt-binary version silently drifts. Uses github-releases (NOT docker datasource) to avoid config:best-practices' docker:pinDigests trying to digest-pin a bare version literal; the `caddy` group rule below joins it with the FROM lines into one PR.

Track the C4-PlantUML stdlib version in the `!include` URLs of docs/diagrams/*.puml. A bump changes the rendered diagrams' macro library, so the committed PNGs MUST be regenerated (`make diagrams`) on the bump PR — diagrams-check (in static-check, which the ci.yml changes-filter re-includes .puml into) blocks the merge until they are.

packageRules.description: Wait 3 days before automerging major updates
Group GitHub Actions into one PR
Group Docker base images (Dockerfiles AND custom-regex docker-datasource pins like CLOUD_PROVIDER_KIND_VERSION / MERMAID_CLI_VERSION / KIND_NODE_IMAGE)

Makefile docker-datasource pins (MERMAID_CLI_VERSION, CLOUD_PROVIDER_KIND_VERSION) are tracked by tag only. Disable digest pinning for them so a pinDigest update cannot collide-drop the version bump within the shared Docker group (config:best-practices enables docker:pinDigests globally). matchManagers is intentionally omitted — the custom-regex manager appears as 'regex', not 'custom.regex', and would AND-void the rule.
Group mise + Makefile tool versions into one PR (excluding docker datasources, which are claimed by the Docker group above)

Group pnpm bumps across managers. pnpm is mirrored in .mise.toml (mise manager) and in package.json#packageManager (npm manager). Without this rule, an upstream pnpm release opens two PRs whose CI green-states are independent — either can land alone, drifting the two pins. Single-group merge keeps them aligned. Placed AFTER the mise-and-Makefile group so this rule's groupName wins for pnpm specifically (later rules override earlier ones for the same property).

Group the Node version across managers. Node is mirrored in .mise.toml/.node-version (mise manager, datasource node-version, major 24) and in the node:NN FROM lines of Dockerfile/Dockerfile.prod (dockerfile manager, datasource docker, 24.16.0). Without this rule a Node bump opens independent PRs that can land separately and drift the pins (make check-node-alignment then reds CI). Placed LAST so this groupName wins for node over the Docker dependencies group.

Group the caddy version across the Dockerfile.prod FROM digests (dockerfile manager, depName `caddy`) and the `xcaddy build vX.Y.Z` rebuild literal (custom.regex, depName `caddyserver/caddy`) so the rebuilt binary version never drifts from the runtime base. Placed LAST so this groupName wins for caddy over the Docker dependencies group; the FROM lines keep docker:pinDigests (only their datasource is docker), the xcaddy literal does not (github-releases).

Hold each new aqua: tool version for 3 days. The aqua: pins (trivy, kind, kubectl, gitleaks, act, hadolint, container-structure-test, yq) resolve via the github-tags datasource, and mise's aqua: backend installs from the GitHub Release — which upstreams publish AFTER the git tag. A 3-day buffer lets the Release artifact publish before the PR opens, preventing the tag-before-publish 404 in `mise install`. The major-only buffer does NOT cover aqua minor/patch bumps (e.g. trivy 0.71.0->0.71.1).

Throttle the local Renovate CLI pin (RENOVATE_VERSION in the Makefile, custom.regex manager / npm datasource). Renovate publishes several times a day; the manager-scoped npm.minimumReleaseAge does not apply to a custom.regex pin, so a 7-day buffer keeps this dev-only tool roughly weekly instead of opening a near-daily self-bump PR.
